figma-console.description: Figma Console MCP — WebSocket bridge to the Figma desktop app. Requires the Figma Desktop Bridge plugin installed in Figma desktop. Primary Figma path — can expand component instance children, resolve variable bindings, reach internal layout properties. Fallback = Figma REST API (-0.30 GIGO penalty). See standards/figma-canary.md for the liveness check protocol.

context7.description: Context7 MCP — up-to-date library documentation. Used by Library Researcher to audit dependency API surfaces. An --api-key (from context7.com/dashboard) can be appended to args for higher rate limits; not strictly required for basic usage.

_comments.ds-adapter-note: Design-system-specific MCP servers (DS tokens, DS components, etc.) are wired in by the active DS adapter's mcp.json, not here. See standards/ds-adapter.md. The adapter's MCP block is merged into this file at runtime.
_comments.figma-rest-note: There is no REST-API MCP server configured by default. The Design Analyst skill invokes REST directly via fetch when the Figma Console MCP canary fails. This keeps the fallback path explicit and GIGO-penalty-tracked rather than silently invisible.
_comments.adding-api-key: To add an optional Context7 API key, change args to ["-y", "@upstash/context7-mcp", "--api-key", "YOUR_KEY"]. Storing the key in env and using process.env in args is not supported in Claude Code MCP config; inline the key or use a secrets manager that rewrites .mcp.json at install time.
